{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UJXwqmqPZQTwKqPqfjnpJq","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→package schemaversion\n     2→\n     3→import \"context\"\n     4→\n     5→// V38 migrates table panel configuration from displayMode to the structured cellOptions format.\n     6→//\n     7→// This migration addresses limitations in the original table panel cell display configuration where\n     8→// the flat displayMode string property could not accommodate the growing complexity of cell rendering\n     9→// options. The original design forced all display settings into a single string value, making it\n    10→// difficult to add new customization parameters or provide mode-specific configuration options.\n    11→//\n    12→// The migration works by:\n    13→// 1. Locating table panels in the dashboard (including nested panels within rows)\n    14→// 2. Examining field configuration defaults and any field overrides for displayMode properties\n    15→// 3. Converting string displayMode values to structured cellOptions objects with type and mode\n    16→// 4. Updating both field defaults and field override references to use the new property path\n    17→// 5. Preserving all existing visual behavior while enabling future cell customization features\n    18→//\n    19→// This restructuring provides several key benefits:\n    20→// - Enables mode-specific configuration options (e.g., gauge thresholds, color schemes)\n    21→// - Supports future cell rendering types without breaking existing configurations\n    22→// - Provides clearer separation between cell type and rendering mode\n    23→// - Maintains backward compatibility while preparing for enhanced table functionality\n    24→//\n    25→// The migration handles special cases for legacy gauge modes and color background variants,\n    26→// ensuring all existing display behaviors are preserved exactly.\n    27→//\n    28→// Example transformations:\n    29→//\n    30→// Before migration (field defaults):\n    31→//\n    32→//\tfieldConfig: {\n    33→//\t  defaults: {\n    34→//\t    custom: {\n    35→//\t      displayMode: \"gradient-gauge\"\n    36→//\t    }\n    37→//\t  }\n    38→//\t}\n    39→//\n    40→// After migration (field defaults):\n    41→//\n    42→//\tfieldConfig: {\n    43→//\t  defaults: {\n    44→//\t    custom: {\n    45→//\t      cellOptions: {\n    46→//\t        type: \"gauge\",\n    47→//\t        mode: \"gradient\"\n    48→//\t      }\n    49→//\t    }\n    50→//\t  }\n    51→//\t}\n    52→//\n    53→// Before migration (field override):\n    54→//\n    55→//\toverrides: [{\n    56→//\t  matcher: { id: \"byName\", options: \"CPU\" },\n    57→//\t  properties: [{\n    58→//\t    id: \"custom.displayMode\",\n    59→//\t    value: transforming array of library elements to map\n\tlibraryElements := generatedDash.Get(\"__elements\")\n\tlibElementsArr, err := libraryElements.Array()\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\telementMap := map[string]any{}\n\t\tfor _, el := range libElementsArr {\n\t\t\tlibElement := simplejson.NewFromAny(el)\n\t\t\telementMap[libElement.Get(\"uid\").MustString()] = el\n\t\t}\n\t\tlibraryElements = simplejson.NewFromAny(elementMap)\n\t}\n\n\t// No need to keep these in the stored dashboard JSON\n\tgeneratedDash.Del(\"__elements\")\n\tgeneratedDash.Del(\"__inputs\")\n\tgeneratedDash.Del(\"__requires\")\n\n\tmetrics.MFolderIDsServiceCount.WithLabelValues(metrics.DashboardImport).Inc()\n\t// here we need to get FolderId from FolderUID if it present in the request, if both exist, FolderUID would overwrite FolderID\n\tif req.FolderUid != \"\" {\n\t\tfolder, err := s.folderService.Get(ctx, &folder.GetFolderQuery{\n\t\t\tOrgID:        req.User.GetOrgID(),\n\t\t\tUID:          &req.FolderUid,\n\t\t\tSignedInUser: req.User,\n\t\t})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\t// nolint:staticcheck\n\t\treq.FolderId = folder.ID\n\t} else {\n\t\tfolder, err := s.folderService.Get(ctx, &folder.GetFolderQuery{\n\t\t\tID:           &req.FolderId, // nolint:staticcheck\n\t\t\tOrgID:        req.User.GetOrgID(),\n\t\t\tSignedInUser: req.User,\n\t\t})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\treq.FolderUid = folder.UID\n\t}\n\n\tvar userID int64\n\tif id, err := identity.UserIdentifier(req.User.GetID()); err == nil {\n\t\tuserID = id\n\t}\n\n\tsaveCmd := dashboards.SaveDashboardCommand{\n\t\tDashboard: generatedDash,\n\t\tOrgID:     req.User.GetOrgID(),\n\t\tUserID:    userID,\n\t\tOverwrite: req.Overwrite,\n\t\tPluginID:  req.PluginId,\n\t\tFolderID:  req.FolderId, // nolint:staticcheck\n\t\tFolderUID: req.FolderUid,\n\t}\n\n\tdto := &dashboards.SaveDashboardDTO{\n\t\tOrgID:     saveCmd.OrgID,\n\t\tDashboard: saveCmd.GetDashboardModel(),\n\t\tOverwrite: saveCmd.Overwrite,\n\t\tUser:      req.User,\n\t}\n\n\t// nolint:staticcheck\n\terr = s.libraryPanelService.ImportLibraryPanelsForDashboard(ctx, req.User, libraryElements, generatedDash.Get(\"panels\").MustArray(), req.FolderId, req.FolderUid)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\tsavedDashboard, err := s.dashboardService.ImportDashboard(ctx, dto)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\tmetrics.MFolderIDsServiceCount.WithLabelValues(metrics.DashboardImport).Inc()\n\n\trevision := savedDashboard.Data.Get(\"revision\").MustInt64(0)\n\tmetrics.MFolderIDsServiceCount.WithLabelValues(metrics.DashboardImport).Inc()\n\treturn &dashboardimport.ImportDashboardResponse{\n\t\tUID:              savedDashboard.UID,\n\t\tPluginId:         req.PluginId,\n\t\tTitle:            savedDashboard.Title,\n\t\tPath:             req.Path,\n\t\tRevision:         revision,                // only used for plugin version tracking\n\t\tFolderId:         savedDashboard.FolderID, // nolint:staticcheck\n\t\tFolderUID:        req.FolderUid,\n\t\tImportedUri:      \"db/\" + savedDashboard.Slug,\n\t\tImportedUrl:      savedDashboard.GetURL(),\n\t\tImportedRevision: revision,\n\t\tImported:         true,\n\t\tDashboardId:      savedDashboard.ID,\n\t\tSlug:             savedDashboard.Slug,\n\t}, nil\n}\n","numLines":176,"startLine":1,"totalLines":176}}}

149→\t\t\t\"list\": []interface{}{},\n   150→\t\t}\n   151→\t}\n   152→}\n   153→\n   154→// ensurePanelsHaveUniqueIds ensures all panels have unique IDs\n   155→func 166→\t\t\tif ids[panelID] {\n   167→\t\t\t\t// Duplicate ID found, assign new one\n   168→\t\t\t\tpanel[\"id\"] = float64(nextPanelId)\n   169→\t\t\t\tnextPanelId++\n   170→\t\t\t} else {\n   171→\t\t\t\t// Valid unique ID, keep it\n   172→\t\t\t\tids[panelID] = true\n   173→\t\t\t}\n   174→\t\t} else {\n   175→\t\t\t// No ID or invalid ID, assign new one\n   176→\t\t\tpanel[\"id\"] = []map[string]interface{}) int {\n   184→\tmax := 0\n   185→\tfor _, panel := range panels {\n   186→\t\tif panelID, ok := panel[\"id\"].(float64); ok && panelID > 0 {\n   187→\t\t\tif int(panelID) > 202→\tsort.SliceStable(panels, func(i, j int) bool {\n   203→\t\tpanelA := panels[i]\n   204→\t\tpanelB := panels[j]\n   205→\n   206→\t\tgridPosA, okA := panelA[\"gridPos\"].(map[string]interface{})\n   207→\t\tgridPosB, okB := panelB[\"gridPos\"].(map[string]interface{})\n   208→\n   209→\t\tif !okA || !okB {\n   210→\t\t\treturn false\n   211→\t\t}\n   212→\n   213→\t\tyA, okA := gridPosA[\"y\"].(float64)\n   214→\t\tyB, okB := gridPosB[\"y\"].(float64)\n   215→\n   216→\t\tif !okA || !okB {\n   217→\t\t\treturn false\n   218→\t\t}\n   219→\n {\n   226→\t\t\t\treturn false\n   227→\t\t\t}\n   228→\n   229→\t\t\treturn xA < xB\n   230→\t\t}\n   231→\n   232→\t\treturn yA < yB\n   233→\t})\n   234→}\n   235→\n   236→// current, ok := variable[\"current\"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n   335→\t\t\t\tif value, exists := current[\"value\"]; exists {\n   336→\t\t\t\t\t// Check for null value\n   337→\t\t\t\t\tif value == nil {\n   338→\t\t\t\t\t\tdelete(current, \"value\")\n   339→\t\t\t\t\t} else if valueArray, isArray := value.([]interface{}); isArray {\n   340→\t\t\t\t\t\t// Check for null values in arrays\n   341→\t\t\t\t\t\thasNull := false\n   342→\t\t\t\t\t\tfor _, v := range valueArray {\n   343→\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif v == nil {\n   344→\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\thasNull = true\n 352→\t\t\t\t}\n   353→\t\t\t}\n   354→\t\t}\n   355→\t}\n   356→}\n   357→\n   358→// ensureQueryIds ensures all queries have refId\n   359→func ensureQueryIds(panel map[string]interface{}) {\n 408→\t}\n   409→}\n   410→\n   411→// getPanels extracts all panels from the dashboard (including nested panels)\n   412→// This matches the frontend's depth-first iteration order in panelIterator()\n   413→func getPanels(dashboard map[string]interface{}) []map[string]interface{} {\n   414→\tvar panels []map[string]interface{}\n   415→\n   416→\t// Get top-level panels\n   417→\tif dashboardPanels, essential properties\n   460→\t\tfor key := range panel {\n   461→\t\t\tdelete(panel, key)\n   462→\t\t}\n   463→\t\tfor key, value := range essentialProps {\n   464→\t\t\tif value != nil {\n (notPersistedProperties)\n   480→\tnotPersistedProps := map[string]bool{\n   481→\t\t\"events\":                  true,\n   482→\t\t\"isViewing\":               true,\n   483→\t\t\"isEditing\": 499→\n   500→\t// Default values that should be filtered out if they match (defaults)\n   501→\tdefaults := map[string]interface{}{\n   502→\t\t\"gridPos\": map[string]interface{}{\n   503→\t\t\t\"x\": float64(0), \"y\": float64(0), \"h\": float64(3), \"w\": float64(6),\n   504→\t\t},\n   505→\t\t\"targets\": []interface{}{\n   506→\t\t\tmap[string]interface{}{\"refId\": \"A\"},\n   507→\t\t},\n   508→\t\t\"cachedPluginOptions\": map[string]interface{}{},\n   509→\t\t\"transparent\":         false,\n   510→\t\t\"options\":             map[string]interface{}{},\n   511→\t\t\"links\":               []interface{}{},\n   512→\t\t\"fieldConfig\": map[string]interface{}{\n   513→\t\t\t\"defaults\":  map[string]interface{}{},\n   514→\t\t\t\"overrides\": []interface{}{},\n   515→\t\t},\n   516→\t\t\"title\": \"\",\n   517→\t}\n   518→\n   519→\t// Remove notPersistedProperties\n   520→\tfor prop := range notPersistedProps {\n   521→\t\tdelete(panel, prop)\n   522→\t}\n   523→\n   524→\t// Filter out properties that match defaults\n   525→\tfor prop, defaultValue := range defaults {\n   526→\t\tif panelValue, exists := panel[prop]; exists {\n panel had them originally\n   534→\tif transformations, ok := panel[\"transformations\"].([]interface{}); ok && len(transformations) == 0 {\n   535→\t\tif panel[\"_originallyHadTransformations\"] != map[string]interface{}) []map[string]interface{} {\n\tvar panels []map[string]interface{}\n\n\t// Get top-level panels\n\tif dashboardPanels, ok := dashboard[\"panels\"].([]interface{}); ok {\n\t\tfor panel)\n\n\t\t\t\t// Then add its nested panels (depth-first order)\n\t\t\t\tif nestedPanels, ok := panel[\"panels\"].([]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tfor _, nestedPanelInterface := range nestedPanels {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif nestedPanel, ok := nestedPanelInterface.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tpanels = append(panels, nestedPanel)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn panels\n}\n\n// cleanupPanelForSaveWithContext mimics the PanelModel.getSaveModel() behavior\n// This removes properties that shouldn't be persisted and filters out default values\nfunc cleanupPanelForSaveWithContext(panel map[string]interface{}, isNested bool) {\n\t// Apply auto-migration logic (matches frontend PanelModel constructor)\n\t// This happens during cleanup phase to match when frontend applies auto-migration\n\t// Only apply auto-migration to top-level panels, not nested ones (matches frontend behavior)\n\tif !isNested {\n\t\tapplyPanelAutoMigration(panel)\n\t}\n\n\t// Library panel specific cleanup (matches frontend behavior)\n\t// Frontend only preserves id, title, gridPos, and libraryPanel for library panels\n\tif libraryPanel, hasLibraryPanel := panel[\"libraryPanel\"]; hasLibraryPanel && libraryPanel != nil {\n\t\t// Create a new panel with only the essential properties\n\t\tessentialProps := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"id\":           panel[\"id\"],\n\t\t\t\"title\":        panel[\"title\"],\n\t\t\t\"gridPos\":      panel[\"gridPos\"],\n\t\t\t\"libraryPanel\": libraryPanel,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Clear the original panel and copy back only essential properties\n\t\tfor key := range panel {\n\t\t\tdelete(panel, key)\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor key, value := range essentialProps {\n\t\t\tif value != nil {\n\t\t\t\tpanel[key] = value\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn // Skip the rest of the cleanup for library panels\n\t}\n\n\t// Row panel specific cleanup (matches frontend behavior)\n\tcleanupRowPanelProperties(panel)\n\n\t// Track which properties were present in the input to preserve them even if they become empty\n\toriginalProperties := make(map[string]bool)\n\tfor key := range panel {\n\t\toriginalProperties[key] = true\n\t}\n\t// Properties that should never be persisted (notPersistedProperties)\n\tnotPersistedProps := map[string]bool{\n\t\t\"events\":                  true,\n\t\t\"isViewing\":               true,\n\t\t\"isEditing\":               true,\n\t\t\"isInView\":                true,\n\t\t\"hasRefreshed\":            true,\n\t\t\"cachedPluginOptions\":     true, // This is the key one causing issues\n\t\t\"plugin\":                  true,\n\t\t\"queryRunner\":             true,\n\t\t\"replaceVariables\":        true,\n\t\t\"configRev\":               true,\n\t\t\"hasSavedPanelEditChange\": true,\n\t\t\"getDisplayTitle\":         true,\n\t\t\"dataSupport\":             true,\n\t\t\"key\":                     true,\n\t\t\"isNew\":                   true,\n\t\t\"refreshWhenInView\":       true,\n\t\t\"scopedVars\":              true, // Frontend removes scopedVars from save model\n\t}\n\n\t// Default values that should be filtered out if they match (defaults)\n\tdefaults := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"gridPos\": map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"x\": float64(0), \"y\": float64(0), \"h\": float64(3), \"w\": float64(6),\n\t\t},\n\t\t\"targets\": []interface{}{\n\t\t\tmap[string]interface{}{\"refId\": \"A\"},\n\t\t},\n\t\t\"cachedPluginOptions\": map[string]interface{}{},\n\t\t\"transparent\":         false,\n\t\t\"options\":             map[string]interface{}{},\n\t\t\"links\":               []interface{}{},\n\t\t\"fieldConfig\": map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"defaults\":  map[string]interface{}{},\n\t\t\t\"overrides\": []interface{}{},\n\t\t},\n\t\t\"title\": \"\",\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove notPersistedProperties\n\tfor prop := range notPersistedProps {\n\t\tdelete(panel, prop)\n\t}\n\n\t// Filter out properties that match defaults\n\tfor prop, defaultValue := range defaults {\n\t\tif panelValue, exists := panel[prop]; exists {\n\t\t\tif isEqual(panelValue, defaultValue) {\n\t\t\t\tdelete(panel, prop)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove empty transformations array unless nested panel had them originally\n\tif transformations, ok := panel[\"transformations\"].([]interface{}); ok && len(transformations) == 0 {\n\t\tif panel[\"_originallyHadTransformations\"] != true || !isNested {\n\t\t\tdelete(panel, \"transformations\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove null values recursively to match frontend's JSON.stringify/parse behavior\n\t// Pass panel type information to help with threshold handling\n\tpanelType := \"\"\n\tif t, ok := panel[\"type\"].(string); ok {\n\t\tpanelType = t\n\t}\n\tremoveNullValuesRecursivelyWithContext(panel, panelType)\n\n\t// Filter out properties that match defaults (matches frontend's isEqual logic)\n\tfilterDefaultValues(panel, originalProperties)\n\n\t// Clean up internal markers\n\tdelete(panel, \"_originallyHadTransformations\")\n\tdelete(panel, \"_originallyHadFieldConfigCustom\")\n}\n\n// filterDefaultValues removes properties that match the default values (matches frontend's isEqual logic)\nfunc filterDefaultValues(panel map[string]interface{}, originalProperties map[string]bool) {\n\t// Get panel type for panel-specific defaults\n\tpanelType := \"\"\n\tif t, ok := panel[\"type\"].(string); ok {\n\t\tpanelType = t\n\t}\n\n\t// PanelModel defaults from frontend\n\tdefaults := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"gridPos\": map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"x\": 0, \"y\": 0, \"h\": 3, \"w\": 6,\n\t\t},\n\t\t\"targets\": []interface{}{\n\t\t\tmap[string]interface{}{\"refId\": \"A\"},\n\t\t},\n\t\t\"cachedPluginOptions\": map[string]interface{}{},\n\t\t\"transparent\":         false,\n\t\t\"options\":             map[string]interface{}{},\n\t\t\"links\":               []interface{}{},\n\t\t\"fieldConfig\": map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"defaults\":  map[string]interface{}{},\n\t\t\t\"overrides\": []interface{}{},\n\t\t},\n\t\t\"title\": \"\",\n\t}\n\n\t// Add panel-specific defaults\n\tif panelType == \"table\" {\n\t\t// Remove legacy table properties (matches frontend getSaveModel filtering)\n\t\t// Exception: preserve original properties for old table panels with autoMigrateFrom=\"table-old\"\n\t\tlegacyTableProps := []string{\"pageSize\", \"scroll\", \"fontSize\", \"showHeader\", \"sort\"}\n\t\tfor _, prop := range legacyTableProps {\n\t\t\tif _, exists := panel[prop]; exists {\n\t\t\t\tif autoMigrateFrom, hasAutoMigrate := panel[\"autoMigrateFrom\"]; hasAutoMigrate && autoMigrateFrom == \"table-old\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tif !originalProperties[prop] {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tdelete(panel, prop)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tdelete(panel, prop)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove properties that match defaults, but preserve properties that were originally present\n\tfor prop, defaultValue := range defaults {\n\t\tif panelValue, exists := panel[prop]; exists {\n\t\t\tif isEqual(panelValue, defaultValue) {\n\t\t\t\t// Special case: fieldConfig - handle preservation of original custom object first\n\t\t\t\tif prop == \"fieldConfig\" {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Check if we need to preserve the custom object before removing fieldConfig\n\t\t\t\t\tif panel[\"_originallyHadFieldConfigCustom\"] == true {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// Ensure fieldConfig structure exists with custom object\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif fieldConfig, ok := panelValue.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif defaults, ok := fieldConfig[\"defaults\"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif _, hasCustom := defaults[\"custom\"]; !hasCustom {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tdefaults[\"custom\"] = map[string]interface{}{}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t// Don't remove fieldConfig if we added custom back\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tdelete(panel, prop)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Only remove if it wasn't originally present in the input\n\t\t\t\t\tif !originalProperties[prop] {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tdelete(panel, prop)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove empty targets arrays (frontend removes them in cleanup)\n\tremoveIfDefaultValue(panel, \"targets\", []interface{}{})\n\n\t// Handle case where fieldConfig was removed but originally had custom object\n\tif panel[\"_originallyHadFieldConfigCustom\"] == true {\n\t\tif _, hasFieldConfig := panel[\"fieldConfig\"]; !hasFieldConfig {\n\t\t\t// Recreate fieldConfig with custom object\n\t\t\tpanel[\"fieldConfig\"] = map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"defaults\": map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\t\"custom\": map[string]interface{}{},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\"overrides\": []interface{}{},\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Clean up fieldConfig to match frontend behavior\n\tif fieldConfig, exists := panel[\"fieldConfig\"].(map[string]interface{}); exists {\n\t\t// Clean up fieldConfig defaults to match frontend behavior\n\t\tif defaults, hasDefaults := fieldConfig[\"defaults\"].(map[string]interface{}); hasDefaults {\n\t\t\t// Remove properties that frontend considers as defaults and omits\n\t\t\tcleanupFieldConfigDefaults(defaults, panel)\n\n\t\t\t// Preserve custom object if it was originally present, even if empty\n\t\t\tif panel[\"_originallyHadFieldConfigCustom\"] == true {\n\t\t\t\tif _, hasCustom := defaults[\"custom\"]; !hasCustom {\n\t\t\t\t\tdefaults[\"custom\"] = map[string]interface{}{}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// isEqual checks if two values are equal (simplified version)\nfunc isEqual(a, b interface{}) bool {\n\tif a == nil && b == nil {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\tif a == nil || b == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// For simple types, use direct comparison\n\tswitch aVal := a.(type) {\n\tcase bool:\n\t\tif bVal, ok := b.(bool); ok {\n\t\t\treturn aVal == bVal\n\t\t}\n\tcase string:\n\t\tif bVal, ok := b.(string); ok {\n\t\t\treturn aVal == bVal\n\t\t}\n\tcase float64:\n\t\tif bVal, ok := b.(float64); ok {\n\t\t\treturn aVal == bVal\n\t\t}\n\tcase []interface{}:\n\t\tif bVal, ok := b.([]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\tif len(aVal) != len(bVal) {\n\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfor i, v := range aVal {\n\t\t\t\tif !isEqual(v, bVal[i]) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n\t\tif bVal, ok := b.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\tif len(aVal) != len(bVal) {\n\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfor k, v := range aVal {\n\t\t\t\tif !isEqual(v, bVal[k]) {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// cleanupDashboardForSave applies the same cleanup logic as the frontend\nfunc cleanupDashboardForSave(dashboard map[string]interface{}) {\n\tremoveNonPersistedProperties(dashboard)\n\tremoveNullValues(dashboard)\n\tcleanupTemplating(dashboard)\n\tcleanupPanels(dashboard)\n\tcleanupDashboardDefaults(dashboard)\n}\n\n// removeNonPersistedProperties removes non-persisted dashboard properties\nfunc removeNonPersistedProperties(dashboard map[string]interface{}) {\n\tnonPersistedProperties := map[string]bool{\n\t\t\"events\":                           true,\n\t\t\"meta\":                             true,\n\t\t\"panels\":                           true, // handled specially below\n\t\t\"templating\":                       true, // handled specially below\n\t\t\"originalTime\":                     true,\n\t\t\"originalTemplating\":               true,\n\t\t\"originalLibraryPanels\":            true,\n\t\t\"panelInEdit\":                      true,\n\t\t\"panelInView\":                      true,\n\t\t\"getVariablesFromState\":            true,\n\t\t\"formatDate\":                       true,\n\t\t\"appEventsSubscription\":            true,\n\t\t\"panelsAffectedByVariableChange\":   true,\n\t\t\"lastRefresh\":                      true,\n\t\t\"timeRangeUpdatedDuringEditOrView\": true,\n\t\t\"originalDashboard\":                true,\n\t}\n\n\tfor k, v := range nonPersistedProperties {\n\t\t// Do not remove \"panels\" and \"templating\" here, as they are handled specially\n\t\tif (k == \"panels\" || k == \"templating\") && v {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif v {\n\t\t\tdelete(dashboard, k)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove properties that frontend omits in getSaveModel\n\tdelete(dashboard, \"variables\")\n}\n\n// removeNullValues removes null values to match frontend's JSON.stringify/parse behavior\nfunc removeNullValues(dashboard map[string]interface{}) {\n\t// This handles gnetId: null and other null properties\n\tremoveIfDefaultValue(dashboard, \"gnetId\", nil)\n}\n\n// cleanupTemplating cleans up templating to match frontend's getTemplatingSaveModel behavior\nfunc cleanupTemplating(dashboard map[string]interface{}) {\n\tif templating, ok := dashboard[\"templating\"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\tremoveNullValuesRecursively(templating)\n\t\tcleanupTemplatingVariables(templating)\n\t}\n}\n\n// cleanupTemplatingVariables applies variable adapter logic\nfunc cleanupTemplatingVariables(templating map[string]interface{}) {\n\tif list, ok := templating[\"list\"].([]interface{}); ok {\n\t\tfor _, variableInterface := range list {\n\t\t\tif variable, ok := variableInterface.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\tcleanupVariable(variable)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// cleanupVariable cleans up individual variable properties\nfunc cleanupVariable(variable map[string]interface{}) {\n\t// Remove null datasource\n\tremoveIfDefaultValue(variable, \"datasource\", nil)\n\n\t// Remove properties that frontend omits in getSaveModel\n\tdelete(variable, \"index\")\n\n\t// Apply variable type-specific logic\n\tif variableType, ok := variable[\"type\"].(string); ok {\n\t\tswitch variableType {\n\t\tcase \"query\":\n\t\t\t// Query variables: keep options: [] if refresh !== never (matches frontend getSaveModel logic)\n\t\t\trefresh := schemaversion.GetIntValue(variable, \"refresh\", 1) // Default to 1 (onDashboardLoad) if not specified\n\t\t\tif refresh != 0 {                                            // 0 = VariableRefreshNever\n\t\t\t\tif _, hasOptions := variable[\"options\"]; !hasOptions {\n\t\t\t\t\tvariable[\"options\"] = []interface{}{}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase \"constant\":\n\t\t\t// Constant variables: remove options completely\n\t\t\tdelete(variable, \"options\")\n\t\tcase \"datasource\":\n\t\t\t// Datasource variables: always set options to empty array\n\t\t\tvariable[\"options\"] = []interface{}{}\n\t\tcase \"custom\":\n\t\t\t// Custom variables: no special handling (just return rest)\n\t\t\t// This is the default behavior - no additional processing needed\n\t\tcase \"textbox\":\n\t\t\t// Textbox variables: handle query vs originalQuery logic\n\t\t\t// For now, just return rest (no special handling needed for basic cases)\n\t\tcase \"adhoc\":\n\t\t\t// Adhoc variables: no special handling\n\t\t\t// This is the default behavior - no additional processing needed\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// cleanupPanels cleans up panels and ensures panels property always exists\nfunc cleanupPanels(dashboard map[string]interface{}) {\n\tif panels, ok := dashboard[\"panels\"].([]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t// Filter out repeated panels (matches frontend getPanelSaveModels behavior)\n\t\t// Frontend filters: !(panel.repeatPanelId || panel.repeatedByRow)\n\t\tfilteredPanels := []interface{}{}\n\t\tfor _, panelInterface := range panels {\n\t\t\tif panel, ok := panelInterface.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\t// Skip panels with repeatPanelId or repeatedByRow\n\t\t\t\tif _, hasRepeatPanelId := panel[\"repeatPanelId\"]; hasRepeatPanelId {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif _, hasRepeatedByRow := panel[\"repeatedByRow\"]; hasRepeatedByRow {\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tfilteredPanels = append(filteredPanels, panel)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tcleanupPanelList(filteredPanels)\n\t\tsortPanelsByGridPosition(filteredPanels)\n\t\tdashboard[\"panels\"] = filteredPanels\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Ensure panels property exists even if empty (matches frontend behavior)\n\t\tdashboard[\"panels\"] = []interface{}{}\n\t}\n}\n\n// cleanupPanelList cleans up all panels including nested ones\nfunc cleanupPanelList(panels []interface{}) {\n\tfor _, panelInterface := range panels {\n\t\tif panel, ok := panelInterface.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\tcleanupPanelForSaveWithContext(panel, false)\n\n\t\t\t// Handle nested panels in row panels\n\t\t\tif nestedPanels, ok := panel[\"panels\"].([]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, nestedPanelInterface := range nestedPanels {\n\t\t\t\t\tif nestedPanel, ok := nestedPanelInterface.(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcleanupPanelForSaveWithContext(nestedPanel, true)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// sortPanelsByGridPosition sorts panels by grid position (matches frontend sortPanelsByGridPos behavior)\nfunc sortPanelsByGridPosition(panels []interface{}) {\n\tsort.SliceStable(panels, func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\tpanelA, okA := panels[i].(map[string]interface{})\n\t\tpanelB, okB := panels[j].(map[string]interface{})\n\t\tif !okA || !okB {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Get gridPos or use default values if missing\n\t\tgridPosA, okA := panelA[\"gridPos\"].(map[string]interface{})\n\t\tgridPosB, okB := panelB[\"gridPos\"].(map[string]interface{})\n\n\t\t// Default gridPos values (matches frontend PanelModel defaults)\n\t\tdefaultY := float64(0)\n\t\tdefaultX := float64(0)\n\n\t\tyA := defaultY\n\t\tif okA {\n\t\t\tif y, ok := gridPosA[\"y\"].(float64); ok {\n\t\t\t\tyA = y\n\t\t\t} else if y, ok := gridPosA[\"y\"].(int); ok {\n\t\t\t\tyA = float64(y)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tyB := defaultY\n\t\tif okB {\n\t\t\tif y, ok := gridPosB[\"y\"].(float64); ok {\n\t\t\t\tyB = y\n\t\t\t} else if y, ok := gridPosB[\"y\"].(int); ok {\n\t\t\t\tyB = float64(y)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif yA == yB {\n\t\t\txA := defaultX\n\t\t\tif okA {\n\t\t\t\tif x, ok := gridPosA[\"x\"].(float64); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\txA = x\n\t\t\t\t} else if x, ok := gridPosA[\"x\"].(int); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\txA = float64(x)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\txB := defaultX\n\t\t\tif okB {\n\t\t\t\tif x, ok := gridPosB[\"x\"].(float64); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\txB = x\n\t\t\t\t} else if x, ok := gridPosB[\"x\"].(int); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\txB = float64(x)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn xA < xB\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn yA < yB\n\t})\n}\n\n// cleanupRowPanelProperties removes default row panel properties that frontend filters out\nfunc cleanupRowPanelProperties(panel map[string]interface{}) {\n\tpanelType, ok := panel[\"type\"].(string)\n\tif !ok || panelType != \"row\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Remove repeat if empty string (default value)\n\tremoveIfDefaultValue(panel, \"repeat\", \"\")\n}\n\n// applyPanelAutoMigration applies the same auto-migration logic as the frontend PanelModel constructor\nfunc applyPanelAutoMigration(panel map[string]interface{}) {\n\tpanelType, ok := panel[\"type\"].(string)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tvar newType string\n\n\t// Graph needs special logic as it can be migrated to multiple panels\n\t// Including graphite which was previously migrated to graph in the schema version 2 migration in DashboardMigrator.ts\n\t// but this was a bug because in there graphite was set to graph, but since those migrations run\n\t// after PanelModel.restoreModel where autoMigrateFrom is set, this caused the graph migration to be skipped.\n\t// And this resulted in a dashboard with invalid panels.\n\tif panelType == \"graph\" || panelType == \"graphite\" {\n\t\t// Check xaxis mode for special cases\n\t\tnewType = getGraphAutoMigration(panel)\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Check autoMigrateAngular mapping\n\t\tautoMigrateAngular := map[string]string{\n\t\t\t\"table-old\":                \"table\",\n\t\t\t\"singlestat\":               \"stat\",\n\t\t\t\"grafana-singlestat-panel\": \"stat\",\n\t\t\t\"grafana-piechart-panel\":   \"piechart\",\n\t\t\t\"grafana-worldmap-panel\":   \"geomap\",\n\t\t\t\"natel-discrete-panel\":     \"state-timeline\",\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif mappedType, exists := autoMigrateAngular[panelType]; exists {\n\t\t\tnewType = mappedType\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply auto-migration if a new type was determined\n\tif newType != \"\" {\n\t\tpanel[\"autoMigrateFrom\"] = panelType\n\t\tpanel[\"type\"] = newType\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc getGraphAutoMigration(panel map[string]interface{}) string {\n\tnewType := \"\"\n\tif xaxis, ok := panel[\"xaxis\"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\tif mode, ok := xaxis[\"mode\"].(string); ok {\n\t\t\tswitch mode {\n\t\t\tcase \"series\":\n\t\t\t\t// Check legend values for bargauge\n\t\t\t\tif legend, ok := panel[\"legend\"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tif values, ok := legend[\"values\"].(bool); ok && values {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tnewType = \"bargauge\"\n\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tnewType = \"barchart\"\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\tnewType = \"barchart\"\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcase \"histogram\":\n\t\t\t\tnewType = \"histogram\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Default graph migration to timeseries\n\tif newType == \"\" {\n\t\tnewType = \"timeseries\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn newType\n}\n\n// removeNullValuesRecursively removes null values from nested objects and arrays\n// This matches the frontend's JSON.stringify/parse behavior\nfunc removeNullValuesRecursively(data interface{}) {\n\tremoveNullValuesRecursivelyWithContext(data, \"\")\n}\n\n// removeNullValuesRecursivelyWithContext removes null values from nested Let me trace through to confirm nested panels in the panels array receive applyPanelDefaults. panels get processed recursively through `cleanupPanelList`, which iterates through each panel and calls `cleanupPanelForSaveWithContext` on it. For row panels that contain nested panels, it also processes those nested panels with a flag indicating they're nested. The nested panels within rows only receive defaults if `applyDefaults` is true—which requires both `showRows` to be true AND the row to not be collapsed. The bug manifests in two related locations:\\n\\n### 1. Primary Bug: filterDefaultValues Function (frontend_defaults.go:611)\\n\\nThe critical flawed conditional is in `filterDefaultValues` at line 611:\\n```go\\nif defaults, ok := fieldConfig[\\\"defaults\\\"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {\\n    if _, hasCustom := defaults[\\\"custom\\\"]; !hasCustom {\\n        defaults[\\\"custom\\\"] = map[string]interface{}{}\\n    }\\n    continue\\n}\\n```\\n\\nWhen `fieldConfig.defaults` is missing (ok=false), the code skips the preservation logic and **falls through to line 620, deleting the entire fieldConfig** even though it should be preserved when `panel[\\\"_originallyHadFieldConfigCustom\\\"] == true`.\\n\\nThis causes field overrides (containing column widths, text alignment, etc.) to be lost during the cleanup phase because the fieldConfig structure that contains them is deleted.\\n\\n### 2. Secondary Issue: processPanelsV38 Function (v38.go:116-131)\\n\\nThe v38 migration has a similar but less problematic conditional structure:\\n```go\\nif defaults, ok := fieldConfig[\\\"defaults\\\"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {\\n    if custom, ok := defaults[\\\"custom\\\"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {\\n        // Migrate displayMode to cellOptions\\n    }\\n}\\nmigrateOverrides(fieldConfig)  // Called regardless\\n```\\n\\nWhile `migrateOverrides` is correctly called outside the conditional (ensuring field overrides are processed), the pattern of checking for defaults.custom presence creates a fragile design that could fail if the defaults structure is incomplete.\\n\\n### 3. Schema Registration (migrations.go:74)\\n\\nV38 is registered in the migration map at line 74 of migrations.go:\\n```go\\n38: V38,\\n```\\n\\n## Impact Flow\\n\\n1. **V38 Migration** (v38.go): Correctly migrates field overrides from `custom.displayMode` to `custom.cellOptions`\\n2. **Cleanup Phase** (frontend_defaults.go:filterDefaultValues): **Incorrectly deletes the fieldConfig** when defaults is missing, causing field overrides to be lost\\n3. **Result**: Field override configuration (column widths, text alignment) is silently dropped for dashboards without explicit `fieldConfig.defaults.custom`\\n\\nThe fix requires modifying the conditional at `frontend_defaults.go:611` to ensure the fieldConfig is preserved even when the defaults structure is missing or incomplete.\"\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":9,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1367,"cache_read_input_tokens":79875,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1367},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3f77f2a9-e660-4172-a9e8-a605615e7d75","uuid":"dbbb53e0-39f5-43bf-a1d8-f7190d68af84"}

Primary Bug: filterDefaultValues Function (frontend_defaults.go:611)\\n\\nThe critical flawed conditional is in `filterDefaultValues` at line 611:\\n```go\\nif defaults, ok := fieldConfig[\\\"defaults\\\"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {\\n    if _, hasCustom := defaults[\\\"custom\\\"]; !hasCustom {\\n        defaults[\\\"custom\\\"] = map[string]interface{}{}\\n    }\\n    continue\\n}\\n```\\n\\nWhen `fieldConfig.defaults` is missing (ok=false), the code skips the preservation logic and **falls through to line 620, deleting the entire fieldConfig** even though it should be preserved when `panel[\\\"_originallyHadFieldConfigCustom\\\"] == true`.\\n\\nThis causes field overrides (containing column widths, text alignment, etc.) to be lost during the cleanup phase because the fieldConfig structure that contains them is deleted.\\n\\n### 2. Primary Bug: filterDefaultValues Function (frontend_defaults.go:611)\\n\\nThe critical flawed conditional is in `filterDefaultValues` at line 611:\\n```go\\nif defaults, ok := fieldConfig[\\\"defaults\\\"].(map[string]interface{}); ok {\\n    if _, hasCustom := defaults[\\\"custom\\\"]; !hasCustom {\\n        defaults[\\\"custom\\\"] = map[string]interface{}{}\\n    }\\n    continue\\n}\\n```\\n\\nWhen `fieldConfig.defaults` is missing (ok=false), the code skips the preservation logic and **falls through to line 620, deleting the entire fieldConfig** even though it should be preserved when `panel[\\\"_originallyHadFieldConfigCustom\\\"] == true`.\\n\\nThis causes field overrides (containing column widths, text alignment, etc.) to be lost during the cleanup phase because the fieldConfig structure that contains them is deleted.\\n\\n### 2. Schema Registration (migrations.go:74)\\n\\nV38 is registered in the migration map at line 74 of migrations.go:\\n```go\\n38: V38,\\n```\\n\\n## Impact Flow\\n\\n1. **V38 Migration** (v38.go): Correctly migrates field overrides from `custom.displayMode` to `custom.cellOptions`\\n2. **Cleanup Phase** (frontend_defaults.go:filterDefaultValues): **Incorrectly deletes the fieldConfig** when defaults is missing, causing field overrides to be lost\\n3. **Result**: Field override configuration (column widths, text alignment) is silently dropped for dashboards without explicit
